strategy_description: EWMA (alpha=0.03) on vega-weighted quadratic IV-in-m_t smile; BS fair per strike; MM on all VEV + extract + slow hydrogel EWMA. Extract MM caused large adverse selection.
Removed standalone VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT quotes; EWMA alpha 0.045 (offline grid tradeoff); book half-spread floor in quote width; smaller hydrogel clips.
Same as v1 plus wing liquidity filter: for strikes 4000/4500/6500, if half-spread >= 8 require |mid−fair| >= 0.85*half_spread before quoting (from tape median wing spreads ~16–21 vs ATM ~3).

Pivot attempt (falsified): added aggressive selective taker leg on tight books when |edge|>=2.0 while keeping sticky EWMA smile + hydrogel MM. This overtraded near-ATM vouchers and caused large losses on days 1-2.
Conservative taker rollback: only near-ATM strikes (5000-5400), tighter spread/edge gates, smaller passive/taker sizes; still underperformed baseline sticky passive approach.
Return to v2 architecture (sticky EWMA smile + wing liquidity filter) with stricter wing edge multiplier=1.0. Performance tied with v2; prefer v2 for simpler calibrated config.

v2 with EWMA-smoothed extract (S_ema) as sole anchor in IV/m_t, smile fit, BS fair, and vega (alpha=0.045, chosen by offline c2-curvature stability grid); raw mids for voucher quotes. Worse PnL than v2 by 9; prefer v2 as parent (simpler, same v2 S tick anchor). DTE: round3description + intraday winding.

Parent v2: slower smile-coeff EWMA (0.03 vs 0.045); wing filter and rest unchanged. Grid search on tape (smile_ewma_fair_drift_5200) found fair-step drift for VEV_5200 nearly identical across EMA rates; backtest total 19255 (tied v2, tie-break keep v2 as canonical config). DTE: round3description + intraday winding.

Parent v2: add BS call gamma at sticky fair_iv; widen quote shift by 0.15*(gamma/GAMMA_REF) with GAMMA_REF from tape medians at VEV_5200/5300. Intended to protect when concavity in S is high; backtest total 19255 (tied v2—integer rounding likely absorbs sub-tick widening). DTE: round3description + intraday winding.

Parent v2 with discrete-impact gamma widening: increased gamma widen scale from 0.15 to 1.0 so bump survives integer quote rounding (analysis-backed). Same sticky EWMA smile and wing filter; underperformed v2 due to weaker day-2 VEV_5400 capture.

Parent v2: double EWMA on smile poly coeffs (fast 0.045 as v2, then slow outer 0.12 on the first EMA; trading uses ema2). Stronger 'sticky' fair without changing S or taker rules. Backtest 19255 (tied v2, tie-break prefer v2 for simpler state). DTE: round3description + intraday winding.

Pivot to family 12 smile_cubic_spline_logk: cubic spline in logk with natural BC, wing regularization by blending node IVs toward quadratic baseline (lambda=0.15), and sticky node-IV EWMA. Architecture kept simple (single pass, no taker leg). Backtest under worse fills produced zero VEV activity and total 18,949.

Family12 distinct variant after duplicate-check: cubic spline logk with synthetic wing anchor nodes (25% beyond observed span) + lighter regularization (lambda=0.10) + faster sticky node EWMA (0.08) + tighter core spread floor. Same no-taker simple architecture.

Family12 propagation-aware variant: cubic-spline logk (wing regularized + anchor nodes) with core-strike shock logic from tape propagation (K=5000..5300: tighter spreads, shock-triggered top-level takes when |dS|>=2 and edge confirms).
v13 with tighter shock gating (CORE_TAKE_EDGE 1.2, CORE_TAKE_SIZE 3, smaller shock size boost) to reduce adverse core-flow while preserving propagation-aware core-vs-wing behavior.
